design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot a transformable mecha through bullet-dense stages, switching between jet and robot modes to navigate hazards and defeat enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mode-switching resource management under fire; players must balance speed (jet) against firepower (robot) while navigating incoming patterns
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control of a variable geometry fighter from the Macross universe, experiencing the iconic transformation mechanic in real-time combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on mode-switching as both a mobility and combat tool rather than a cosmetic gimmick; the transformation system IS the core mechanical identity

difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present (standard for arcade-style SNES shmups)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; no mid-stage checkpoints typical of era
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Mode-switching timing provides skill expression; player must learn optimal transformation patterns for each enemy formation

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage progression; no branching paths
replayability_hooks: Score attack mode (implied standard shmup feature)
Pattern memorization encourages repeated runs for optimization

world_building_techniques: Macross universe IP provides implicit lore context
Stage theming reflects familiar Macross combat scenarios

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based encounters emphasizing mode-switching counterplay; bosses designed to force strategic transformation decisions

extraction_notes: Source provided (tcrf.net) contains no usable game design documentation for Macross: Scrambled Valkyrie. Only generic TCRF navigation information was available. Design analysis derived from genre conventions (1993 SNES shmup) and Macross IP mechanics (transformation system). All values should be treated as contextual inference rather than documented design data.
